story.iWant: the application to periodically check for project updates in the background
story.soThat: I can see changes made by other team members without manually refreshing
story.tasks: - DESCOPED FROM MVP - No implementation work required - This story retained for future Phase 3 planning - Manual refresh implemented in E3-S4 (Project Sidebar UI) instead

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: All acceptance criteria descoped from MVP per Tech Spec Open Question #5
Manual refresh only in MVP (E3-S4, E3-S6)
Automatic polling deferred to Phase 3 (WebSockets or polling-based updates)

doc.section: Open Question #5 Resolution: - DECISION: Manual polling only (no auto-polling in MVP) - Decided by: Architecture review (2025-11-27) - Rationale: Simplifies MVP implementation; users can manually refresh when needed - Implementation: Remove ProjectPollingService from MVP scope (defer to Phase 3) - Add "Refresh" button in project sidebar and detail view - Future: Add auto-polling in Phase 3 when WebSockets implemented

1. User clicks "Refresh" button in project sidebar or detail view 2. ProjectService.refresh() invalidates all project caches 3. Fresh data fetched from Moku API: GET /api/projects, GET /api/projects/{id}/members 4. Cache updated with fresh data (new 5-minute TTL) 5. UI updates to show latest projects, members, threads 6. No automatic polling - refresh is always user-initiated

code.note: NO CODE TO IMPLEMENT - Story descoped from MVP

constraints.constraint: DESCOPED FROM MVP - No implementation required
Phase 3 may implement WebSockets for real-time updates (preferred over polling)
Risk of stale data mitigated by prominent "Refresh" buttons in UI

interfaces.note: NO INTERFACES - Story descoped from MVP

tests.standards: NO TESTS REQUIRED - Story descoped from MVP
tests.locations: N/A - No tests needed for descoped story

ideas.note: For future Phase 3 implementation, consider testing:
